danger_signals.visual_form: Map territories displayed in enemy faction colors on tactics screen
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates regions controlled by opposing families; player must assess military threat from proximity

danger_signals.visual_form: Unit markers on battle screen showing opposing forces
danger_signals.meaning: Visual representation of combat threat; larger concentrations indicate greater danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Resource display on left side of tactics screen
danger_signals.meaning: Army sustenance critical; affects ability to maintain troops in field and win battles

danger_signals.visual_form: Defense upgrade indicators on tactics screen
danger_signals.meaning: Undefended or lightly-defended provinces are vulnerable to enemy attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrative/world-state: Dragon sealed in ruby at crown
danger_signals.meaning: Latent existential threat to island; breaking of magical seal represents danger to all factions

safety_signals.visual_form: Map territories in player's faction color
safety_signals.meaning: Secure zones under player control; bases for operations and resource generation

safety_signals.visual_form: Defense upgrade visual indicators and unit fortifications (fences)
safety_signals.meaning: Territory is hardened against invasion; units can build fences to create barriers

safety_signals.visual_form: Resource counter on tactics screen left panel
safety_signals.meaning: Sufficient food and economic capacity to maintain army and upgrade territories

safety_signals.visual_form: Unit count and type availability on tactics screen
safety_signals.meaning: Player can field five unit types (archers, knights, horsemen, wizard/hired unit); numerical advantage indicates tactical advantage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Led by Prince Erin; allied with Zendor (Emerald Wizard, Lightning magic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Territory control shown on world map; one of two most powerful clans

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Led by Prince Ander; allied with Pluvius (Sapphire Wizard, Meteor magic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Territory control shown on world map; one of two most powerful clans
Minor clan with wizard support; territories visible on tactics screen map
Minor clan with wizard support; territories visible on tactics screen map
Minor clan with wizard support; territories visible on tactics screen map
Minor clan with wizard support; territories visible on tactics screen map

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Tyrannical ruler; originally held the Gemfire crown before Princess Robyn dispersed wizard gems
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Initial ruler of Isle of Ishmeria; represents oppressive old order that player factions unite against

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six wizard gems unified in Gemfire crown; Fire Dragon sealed in ruby
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Island under tyrannical rule; subjects oppressed; peace maintained through fear rather than consent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six wizard gems scattered across island to different family territories; crown broken
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Six competing clans activate simultaneously; each seeks to claim crown and throne; civil war emerges as primary conflict mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tactics screen shows patchwork map with territories colored by controlling faction; multiple provinces per faction distributed across island
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage adjacent territory threats; diplomacy and alliances become strategic options; military expansion requires careful sequencing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One or two factions controlling majority of visible map territories (Blanches/Lyles as strongest initial powers)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dominant factions pose greater conquest threat; weaker factions vulnerable to attack; player must assess when to attack, defend, or ally

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Resource display depleting on tactics screen; low numbers in food/economic indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Army loses capacity to sustain field operations; must prioritize territory economy upgrades; armies can lose battles due to food shortage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player's faction color dominates map; most territories under single control; enemy territories reduced to isolated pockets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Map unification complete; player has conquered entire island; throne claimed; Gemfire restored under player's rule

environmental_storytelling.example: Tactics screen right panel displays world map with each territory colored to show which family controls it; player visually reads power distribution at a glance
Six wizard gems assigned to six clans creates inherent narrative of competing magical powers; each family's control of a gem communicates their military/magical legitimacy
Each territory contains a castle; controlling the castle controls the territory; castle becomes visual anchor and strategic focal point

environmental_storytelling.technique: Unit type variety as faction identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Different unit types (archers, knights, horsemen, wizards, hired units) represent military capabilities; wizard availability tied directly to family's magical authority
Only adjacent territories can be attacked or have diplomatic actions performed; geography naturally creates alliance zones and conflict frontlines visible on map
Limited monthly actions per territory and finite food supply create strategic tension; forces players to choose between offensive expansion and defensive consolidation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Limited territories and resources; must carefully select initial targets; can hire or move troops, or engage in diplomacy rather than immediate conquest
One of two most powerful factions; backed by Zendor (Lightning wizard); occupies prominent starting position; represents primary early-game threat to player
One of two most powerful factions; backed by Pluvius (Meteor wizard); second-most dangerous faction; controls significant territory; poses threat if allied against player
Weaker than Blanches/Lyles; vulnerable to early conquest; represent expansion opportunities; eliminated or vassalized as game progresses
Defeated when castle captured, all units eliminated, or food runs out; uses defensive fences to control battlefield terrain
Alternative to hired units; represents family's magical authority; each wizard uses unique magic type (Lightning, Meteors, etc.); strategic advantage in specific battle conditions
One action per in-game month per territory; can upgrade economy/defenses or engage in military/diplomatic action; constrained by resource availability and adjacent territory access

notes: Gemfire communicates world-state primarily through a two-layer interface: (1) Tactics screen showing resource management (left panel) and territorial control map (right panel), and (2) Battle screen showing individual unit positioning. Faction identity is communicated through gem assignment rather than visual distinctive markers—the narrative establishes that each family 'has' a wizard with a specific gem, making gem type equivalent to faction symbol. Danger and safety are visually communicated through map color distribution: enemy-colored territories indicate threat proximity, while player-colored territories indicate security. The game's political situation is embedded in the map state itself—fragmented control implies ongoing civil war, while consolidated control implies approaching victory. Limited source material prevents extraction of specific color schemes or detailed UI mockups for faction identification.
